titleStmt.title: Addressing Bias and Enhancing Accessibility in Real-Time Digital Archives: Lessons from the Edut 710 Initiative

author.affiliation: The Hebrew University of Jerusalem, Israel
The Hebrew University of Jerusalem, Israel
The Hebrew University of Jerusalem, Israel

keywords.term: I plan to attend the conference on-site in Lisbon

body.p: Cardoni, Giulia / Fabbri, Francesca / Iannucci, Alessandro (2022): “The digital living archive and the construction of a participatory cultural memory in the DARE-UIA project” Bibliothecae. it 11.2 (2022): 350-380.

Colavizza, Giovanni / Blanke, Tobias / Jeurgens, Charles / Noordegraaf Julia (2021): Archives and AI: An Overview of Current Debates and Future Perspectives. Journal on Computing and Cultural Heritage 15, 1, Article 4 (February 2022), 15 pages. >https://doi.org/10.1145/3479010> ]Accessed April 30, 2025[
Kentor, Itay / Keydar, Renana, Edut 710 team (2024): Edut 710 Empowering Voices, Remembrance and Research: Journal of Israel Oral History Assn. Number 5, October 2024. (In Hebrew)
Ketelaar, Eric (2009):, “A Living Archive, Shared by Communities of Records,” in Community Archives: The Shaping of Memory, ed. Ben Alexander and Jeannette A. Bastian (Facet, 2009), 116.
Keydar, Renana (2020): “Listening from Afar: An Algorithmic Analysis of Testimonies from the International Criminal Courts,” Illinois Journal of Law, Technology & Policy 2020, no. 1 (2020): 55–83
Keydar, Renana (2022): “Changing the Lens on Survivor Testimony: Topic Modeling the Eichmann Trial,” Jewish Studies Quarterly, 2022.

Keydar, Renana / Pinchevski, Amit / Ifergan, Maxim / Abend, Omri (2025): The Testimony of the Multitude: Towards a computational model of listening to Holocaust testimony, Holocaust and Genocide Studies (forthcoming 2025).
Netzer, Yael / Keydar, Renana / Shuster, Keren (2024): Structuring a Digital Archive of Survivor Testimony in Times of Emergency, presented at DH2024.
Presner, Todd Samuel (2024): Ethics of the Algorithm: Digital Humanities and Holocaust Memory (Princeton: Princeton University Press, 2024), 10.
Sabiescu, Amalia G. (2020): “Living Archives and The Social Transmission of Memory,” Curator: The Museum Journal 63, no. 4 (October 2020): 497–510, doi:10.1111/cura.12384
